steps.message:
# Marketplace Activity

A company owning an online marketplace wants to better understand supply/demand in its market and run targeted campaigns encouraging users to buy items from a given category. Users are located in different cities. The company is interested in which cities, and for which categories, additional marketing / improved recommendations / free delivery offers, etc., is particularly needed due to the unexpected variability of demand depending on location. They want to find cities where the number of transactions could be further increased using dedicated strategies (such as more notifications like "Buy products from category <X> on our platform!"), as this, for example, is strongly suggested by activity in other cities for the same listing categories.

The question should be answered based on the user activity in different cities. We are interested only in cities with a non-negligible number of users.

We also know that there are different types of users and their behavior depends on which group they belong to. Also, note that according to the company policy, unsold listings automatically expire after 2 months.

Dataset:

- The generated CSV files are available directly in `/app`.

Answer format:

- Write your final answer to `/app/answer.json`.
- The file must contain only a JSON object mapping city names to arrays of category names
- Sort city keys alphabetically.
- Sort category names alphabetically inside each array.

Example:

```json
{"Lyon":["Books","Clothes"],"Paris":["Smartphones"]}
```
I'll start by exploring the dataset files in `/app`.

arguments.command: ls -la /app && for f in /app/*.csv; do echo "=== $f ==="; head -5 "$f"; echo "...rows: $(wc -l < "$f")"; done
arguments.description: List CSV files and preview each

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, glob

users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
listings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('listing_*.csv'))])
purch = pd.read_csv('purchase_001.csv')
clicks = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('click_event_*.csv'))])

print("listings:", len(listings), "purchases:", len(purch), "clicks:", len(clicks))

# join purchase -> buyer info and listing category
purch = purch.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], left_on='buyer_id', right_on='user_id') \
             .merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id','price','created_at','expires_at']], on='listing_id')
clicks = clicks.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id') \
               .merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id']], on='listing_id')

print("\npurchases by buyer user_type:\n", purch.user_type.value_counts())
print("\nclicks by user_type:\n", clicks.user_type.value_counts())

# overall conversion by user type
c_by_type = clicks.groupby('user_type').size()
p_by_type = purch.groupby('user_type').size()
print("\nclick->purchase conversion by type:\n", (p_by_type/c_by_type).round(4))

# user counts by type
u_by_type = users.user_type.value_counts()
print("\nclicks per user by type:\n", (c_by_type/u_by_type).round(2))
print("\npurchases per user by type:\n", (p_by_type/u_by_type).round(2))

# sellers by type
sell = listings.merge(users[['user_id','user_type']], left_on='seller_id', right_on='user_id')
print("\nlistings by seller type:\n", sell.user_type.value_counts())

purch.to_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl'); clicks.to_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
EOF
arguments.description: Join purchases/clicks with users and listings; per-type behavior

raw_arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, glob

users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
listings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('listing_*.csv'))])
purch = pd.read_csv('purchase_001.csv')
clicks = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('click_event_*.csv'))])

print("listings:", len(listings), "purchases:", len(purch), "clicks:", len(clicks))

# join purchase -> buyer info and listing category
purch = purch.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], left_on='buyer_id', right_on='user_id') \
             .merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id','price','created_at','expires_at']], on='listing_id')
clicks = clicks.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id') \
               .merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id']], on='listing_id')

print("\npurchases by buyer user_type:\n", purch.user_type.value_counts())
print("\nclicks by user_type:\n", clicks.user_type.value_counts())

# overall conversion by user type
c_by_type = clicks.groupby('user_type').size()
p_by_type = purch.groupby('user_type').size()
print("\nclick->purchase conversion by type:\n", (p_by_type/c_by_type).round(4))

# user counts by type
u_by_type = users.user_type.value_counts()
print("\nclicks per user by type:\n", (c_by_type/u_by_type).round(2))
print("\npurchases per user by type:\n", (p_by_type/u_by_type).round(2))

# sellers by type
sell = listings.merge(users[['user_id','user_type']], left_on='seller_id', right_on='user_id')
print("\nlistings by seller type:\n", sell.user_type.value_counts())

purch.to_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl'); clicks.to_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
EOF
raw_arguments.description: Join purchases/clicks with users and listings; per-type behavior

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd

purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')

for df, tcol in [(purch,'purchase_time'), (clicks,'timestamp')]:
    df[tcol] = pd.to_datetime(df[tcol])
print("purchase range:", purch.purchase_time.min(), purch.purchase_time.max())
print("click range:", clicks.timestamp.min(), clicks.timestamp.max())

purch['created_at'] = pd.to_datetime(purch['created_at'])
purch['expires_at'] = pd.to_datetime(purch['expires_at'])
print("\npurchases after listing expiry:", (purch.purchase_time > purch.expires_at).sum())
print("purchases before listing created:", (purch.purchase_time < purch.created_at).sum())
print("duplicate listing purchases:", purch.listing_id.duplicated().sum())

# clicks vs expiry
import glob
listings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('listing_*.csv'))])
listings['expires_at'] = pd.to_datetime(listings['expires_at'])
listings['created_at'] = pd.to_datetime(listings['created_at'])
cl = clicks.merge(listings[['listing_id','expires_at','created_at']], on='listing_id')
print("clicks after expiry:", (cl.timestamp > cl.expires_at).sum())
print("expiry-creation gap days:", (listings.expires_at-listings.created_at).dt.days.describe().round(1).to_dict())

# click -> purchase lag
m = purch.merge(clicks[['user_id','listing_id','timestamp']], left_on=['buyer_id','listing_id'], right_on=['user_id','listing_id'], how='left')
lag = (m.purchase_time - m.timestamp).dt.total_seconds()/60
print("\nclick->purchase lag minutes:", lag.describe().round(1).to_dict())
print("purchases without matching click:", m.timestamp.isna().sum())
EOF

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np

purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')

# cities with non-negligible users
city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big_cities = set(city_counts[city_counts >= 50].index)
print(f"{len(big_cities)} cities kept")

clicks_b = clicks[clicks.city.isin(big_cities)]
purch_b = purch[purch.city.isin(big_cities)]

# global conversion per (user_type, category) -- pooled across cities
g_clicks = clicks_b.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('clicks')
g_purch = purch_b.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('purch')
g = pd.concat([g_clicks, g_purch], axis=1).fillna(0)
g['rate'] = g.purch/g.clicks

# per (city, category, type)
cc_clicks = clicks_b.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size().rename('clicks')
cc_purch = purch_b.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size().rename('purch')
cc = pd.concat([cc_clicks, cc_purch], axis=1).fillna(0).reset_index()
cc = cc.merge(g['rate'].reset_index(), on=['user_type','category_id'])
cc['expected'] = cc.clicks * cc.rate

agg = cc.groupby(['city','category_id']).agg(clicks=('clicks','sum'), purch=('purch','sum'), expected=('expected','sum')).reset_index()
agg['ratio'] = agg.purch/agg.expected

# Poisson lower-tail p-value for observing <= purch given expected
from scipy.stats import poisson
agg['p_low'] = poisson.cdf(agg.purch, agg.expected)

agg = agg.merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')
sus = agg[(agg.expected >= 5)].sort_values('p_low')
print(sus.head(40).to_string(index=False))
print("\n#pairs with p_low < 1e-4:", (sus.p_low < 1e-4).sum())
print("#pairs with p_low < 1e-3:", (sus.p_low < 1e-3).sum())
agg.to_pickle('/tmp/agg.pkl')
EOF
arguments.description: Compute expected vs actual purchases per city-category

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np
from scipy.stats import poisson

purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')

# active users only? check inactive activity
inact = set(users[~users.is_active].user_id)
print("clicks by inactive users:", clicks.user_id.isin(inact).sum(), "/", len(clicks))
print("purchases by inactive:", purch.buyer_id.isin(inact).sum(), "/", len(purch))

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)

U = users[users.city.isin(big)]
P = purch[purch.city.isin(big)]

# users per (city, type)
u_ct = U.groupby(['city','user_type']).size().rename('n_users')
# global purchases per user per (type, category): total purchases(type,cat) / total users(type)
tot_u_type = U.groupby('user_type').size()
g_p = P.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('purch').reset_index()
g_p['rate_per_user'] = g_p.purch / g_p.user_type.map(tot_u_type)

# expected per (city, cat) = sum_type n_users(city,type)*rate(type,cat)
grid = u_ct.reset_index().merge(g_p[['user_type','category_id','rate_per_user']], on='user_type')
grid['exp'] = grid.n_users * grid.rate_per_user
exp_cc = grid.groupby(['city','category_id'])['exp'].sum()

act_cc = P.groupby(['city','category_id']).size().rename('purch')
df = pd.concat([exp_cc, act_cc], axis=1).fillna(0).reset_index()
df['ratio'] = df.purch/df['exp']
df['p_low'] = poisson.cdf(df.purch, df['exp'])
df = df.merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')
df = df[df['exp']>=5].sort_values('p_low')
print(df.head(40).to_string(index=False))
print("\n#pairs p<1e-6:", (df.p_low<1e-6).sum(), " p<1e-4:", (df.p_low<1e-4).sum(), " p<1e-3:", (df.p_low<1e-3).sum())
df.to_pickle('/tmp/df_purchuser.pkl')
EOF
arguments.description: Test purchases-per-user deficits by city and category

import pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob

purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')

searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)
U = users[users.city.isin(big)]
tot_u_type = U.groupby('user_type').size()

def per_user_table(events, cat_col, label):
    E = events[events.city.isin(big)]
    g = E.groupby(['user_type',cat_col]).size().rename('n').reset_index()
    g['rate'] = g.n/g.user_type.map(tot_u_type)
    u_ct = U.groupby(['city','user_type']).size().rename('n_users').reset_index()
    grid = u_ct.merge(g[['user_type',cat_col,'rate']], on='user_type')
    grid['exp'] = grid.n_users*grid.rate
    exp_cc = grid.groupby(['city',cat_col])['exp'].sum()
    act = E.groupby(['city',cat_col]).size().rename('act')
    out = pd.concat([exp_cc, act], axis=1).fillna(0)
    out.columns = [f'exp_{label}', f'act_{label}']
    return out

t_search = per_user_table(searches, 'category_id', 'search')
t_click = per_user_table(clicks, 'category_id', 'click')
t_purch = per_user_table(purch, 'category_id', 'purch')

F = pd.concat([t_search, t_click, t_purch], axis=1).fillna(0).reset_index().merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')
F['r_search'] = F.act_search/F.exp_search
F['r_click'] = F.act_click/F.exp_click
F['r_purch'] = F.act_purch/F.exp_purch

top = [('Madrid','Pets'),('Bilbao','Formalwear'),('Barcelona','Art'),('Barcelona','Decor'),
       ('Sevilla','Computers'),('Bilbao',"Women's Shoes"),('Bilbao','Bags'),('Malaga',"Women's Clothing"),
       ('Granada',"Women's Clothing"),('Sevilla','Video Games'),('Zaragoza','Collectibles'),
       ('Santander',"Women's Clothing"),('Valencia','Bicycles'),('Barcelona','Books')]
for c,cat in top:
    row = F[(F.city==c)&(F.category_name==cat)]
    r = row.iloc[0]
    print(f"{c:12s} {cat:18s} search {r.act_search:5.0f}/{r.exp_search:7.1f}={r.r_search:.2f}  click {r.act_click:4.0f}/{r.exp_click:6.1f}={r.r_click:.2f}  purch {r.act_purch:3.0f}/{r.exp_purch:5.1f}={r.r_purch:.2f}")
F.to_pickle('/tmp/funnel.pkl')
EOF

raw_arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob

purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')

searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)
U = users[users.city.isin(big)]
tot_u_type = U.groupby('user_type').size()

def per_user_table(events, cat_col, label):
    E = events[events.city.isin(big)]
    g = E.groupby(['user_type',cat_col]).size().rename('n').reset_index()
    g['rate'] = g.n/g.user_type.map(tot_u_type)
    u_ct = U.groupby(['city','user_type']).size().rename('n_users').reset_index()
    grid = u_ct.merge(g[['user_type',cat_col,'rate']], on='user_type')
    grid['exp'] = grid.n_users*grid.rate
    exp_cc = grid.groupby(['city',cat_col])['exp'].sum()
    act = E.groupby(['city',cat_col]).size().rename('act')
    out = pd.concat([exp_cc, act], axis=1).fillna(0)
    out.columns = [f'exp_{label}', f'act_{label}']
    return out

t_search = per_user_table(searches, 'category_id', 'search')
t_click = per_user_table(clicks, 'category_id', 'click')
t_purch = per_user_table(purch, 'category_id', 'purch')

F = pd.concat([t_search, t_click, t_purch], axis=1).fillna(0).reset_index().merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')
F['r_search'] = F.act_search/F.exp_search
F['r_click'] = F.act_click/F.exp_click
F['r_purch'] = F.act_purch/F.exp_purch

top = [('Madrid','Pets'),('Bilbao','Formalwear'),('Barcelona','Art'),('Barcelona','Decor'),
       ('Sevilla','Computers'),('Bilbao',"Women's Shoes"),('Bilbao','Bags'),('Malaga',"Women's Clothing"),
       ('Granada',"Women's Clothing"),('Sevilla','Video Games'),('Zaragoza','Collectibles'),
       ('Santander',"Women's Clothing"),('Valencia','Bicycles'),('Barcelona','Books')]
for c,cat in top:
    row = F[(F.city==c)&(F.category_name==cat)]
    r = row.iloc[0]
    print(f"{c:12s} {cat:18s} search {r.act_search:5.0f}/{r.exp_search:7.1f}={r.r_search:.2f}  click {r.act_click:4.0f}/{r.exp_click:6.1f}={r.r_click:.2f}  purch {r.act_purch:3.0f}/{r.exp_purch:5.1f}={r.r_purch:.2f}")
F.to_pickle('/tmp/funnel.pkl')
EOF
raw_arguments.description: Funnel decomposition for top suspicious city-category pairs

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np
from scipy.stats import poisson

F = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/funnel.pkl')
F['p_purch'] = poisson.cdf(F.act_purch, F.exp_purch)
F['p_click'] = poisson.cdf(F.act_click, F.exp_click)
F['p_search'] = poisson.cdf(F.act_search, F.exp_search)

# combine click+purchase evidence (Fisher)
from scipy.stats import combine_pvalues
F['p_comb'] = [combine_pvalues([a,b])[1] for a,b in zip(F.p_click, F.p_purch)]

S = F[(F.exp_click>=10)].sort_values('p_comb')
cols=['city','category_name','act_search','exp_search','act_click','exp_click','act_purch','exp_purch','p_click','p_purch','p_comb']
pd.set_option('display.width', 250)
print(S[cols].head(45).to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.3g}"))
print("\np_comb sorted values (top 40):")
print(np.sort(S.p_comb.values)[:40])
print("\nN pairs tested:", len(S))
F.to_pickle('/tmp/funnel.pkl')
EOF
arguments.description: Combined click+purchase deficit significance per pair

python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob

users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
listings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('listing_*.csv'))])
imp = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, usecols=['impression_id','user_id','listing_id','source_type']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('impression_event_*.csv'))])
fav = pd.read_csv('favorite_event_001.csv')
print("impressions:", len(imp))

imp = imp.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id').merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id']], on='listing_id')
fav = fav.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id').merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id']], on='listing_id')
imp.to_pickle('/tmp/imp.pkl'); fav.to_pickle('/tmp/fav.pkl')

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)
U = users[users.city.isin(big)]
tot_u_type = U.groupby('user_type').size()

def per_user(events, label):
    E = events[events.city.isin(big)]
    g = E.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('n').reset_index()
    g['rate'] = g.n/g.user_type.map(tot_u_type)
    u_ct = U.groupby(['city','user_type']).size().rename('n_users').reset_index()
    grid = u_ct.merge(g[['user_type','category_id','rate']], on='user_type')
    grid['exp'] = grid.n_users*grid.rate
    exp_cc = grid.groupby(['city','category_id'])['exp'].sum()
    act = E.groupby(['city','category_id']).size().rename('act')
    out = pd.concat([exp_cc, act], axis=1).fillna(0)
    out.columns = [f'exp_{label}', f'act_{label}']
    return out

t_imp = per_user(imp, 'imp')
t_fav = per_user(fav, 'fav')
F = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/funnel.pkl').set_index(['city','category_id'])
F = F.join(t_imp).join(t_fav).fillna(0)
F['r_imp'] = F.act_imp/F.exp_imp
F['r_fav'] = F.act_fav/F.exp_fav
F.to_pickle('/tmp/funnel2.pkl')

cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
top = [('Madrid',54),('Bilbao',16),('Barcelona',37),('Barcelona',26),('Sevilla',18),('Bilbao',11),('Bilbao',13),
       ('Santander',9),('Zaragoza',19),('Barcelona',33),('Valencia',25),('Madrid',58),('Oviedo',49),('Sevilla',22),
       ('Madrid',29),('Valladolid',19),('Cordoba',21),('Salamanca',56),('Vigo',9),('Murcia',68)]
catname = dict(zip(cats.category_id, cats.category_name))
print(f"{'city':12s} {'category':18s} {'search':>12s} {'impr':>14s} {'click':>12s} {'fav':>10s} {'purch':>10s}")
for c,cid in top:
    r = F.loc[(c,cid)]
    print(f"{c:12s} {catname[cid]:18s} {r.act_search:4.0f}/{r.exp_search:5.0f}={r.r_search:4.2f} {r.act_imp:5.0f}/{r.exp_imp:6.0f}={r.r_imp:4.2f} {r.act_click:4.0f}/{r.exp_click:4.0f}={r.r_click:4.2f} {r.act_fav:3.0f}/{r.exp_fav:4.0f}={r.r_fav:4.2f} {r.act_purch:3.0f}/{r.exp_purch:3.0f}={r.r_purch:4.2f}")
EOF

listings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('listing_*.csv'))])
imp = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, usecols=['impression_id','user_id','listing_id','source_type']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('impression_event_*.csv'))])
fav = pd.read_csv('favorite_event_001.csv')
print("impressions:", len(imp))

imp = imp.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id').merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id']], on='listing_id')
fav = fav.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id').merge(listings[['listing_id','category_id']], on='listing_id')
imp.to_pickle('/tmp/imp.pkl'); fav.to_pickle('/tmp/fav.pkl')

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)
U = users[users.city.isin(big)]
tot_u_type = U.groupby('user_type').size()

def per_user(events, label):
    E = events[events.city.isin(big)]
    g = E.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('n').reset_index()
    g['rate'] = g.n/g.user_type.map(tot_u_type)
    u_ct = U.groupby(['city','user_type']).size().rename('n_users').reset_index()
    grid = u_ct.merge(g[['user_type','category_id','rate']], on='user_type')
    grid['exp'] = grid.n_users*grid.rate
    exp_cc = grid.groupby(['city','category_id'])['exp'].sum()
    act = E.groupby(['city','category_id']).size().rename('act')
    out = pd.concat([exp_cc, act], axis=1).fillna(0)
    out.columns = [f'exp_{label}', f'act_{label}']
    return out

t_imp = per_user(imp, 'imp')
t_fav = per_user(fav, 'fav')
F = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/funnel.pkl').set_index(['city','category_id'])
F = F.join(t_imp).join(t_fav).fillna(0)
F['r_imp'] = F.act_imp/F.exp_imp
F['r_fav'] = F.act_fav/F.exp_fav
F.to_pickle('/tmp/funnel2.pkl')

cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
top = [('Madrid',54),('Bilbao',16),('Barcelona',37),('Barcelona',26),('Sevilla',18),('Bilbao',11),('Bilbao',13),
       ('Santander',9),('Zaragoza',19),('Barcelona',33),('Valencia',25),('Madrid',58),('Oviedo',49),('Sevilla',22),
       ('Madrid',29),('Valladolid',19),('Cordoba',21),('Salamanca',56),('Vigo',9),('Murcia',68)]
catname = dict(zip(cats.category_id, cats.category_name))
print(f"{'city':12s} {'category':18s} {'search':>12s} {'impr':>14s} {'click':>12s} {'fav':>10s} {'purch':>10s}")
for c,cid in top:
    r = F.loc[(c,cid)]
    print(f"{c:12s} {catname[cid]:18s} {r.act_search:4.0f}/{r.exp_search:5.0f}={r.r_search:4.2f} {r.act_imp:5.0f}/{r.exp_imp:6.0f}={r.r_imp:4.2f} {r.act_click:4.0f}/{r.exp_click:4.0f}={r.r_click:4.2f} {r.act_fav:3.0f}/{r.exp_fav:4.0f}={r.r_fav:4.2f} {r.act_purch:3.0f}/{r.exp_purch:3.0f}={r.r_purch:4.2f}")
EOF
raw_arguments.description: Full funnel (search/impression/click/favorite/purchase) for top pairs

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob
from scipy.stats import poisson

users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)

S = searches[searches.city.isin(big)]
P = purch[purch.city.isin(big)]
C = clicks[clicks.city.isin(big)]

# global purchases-per-search and clicks-per-search per (type, category)
s_tc = S.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('s')
p_tc = P.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('p')
c_tc = C.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('c')
G = pd.concat([s_tc,p_tc,c_tc],axis=1).fillna(0)
G['pps'] = G.p/G.s   # purchases per search
G['cps'] = G.c/G.s   # clicks per search

# city-level actuals per (city, cat, type)
s_cct = S.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size().rename('s').reset_index()
s_cct = s_cct.merge(G[['pps','cps']].reset_index(), on=['user_type','category_id'], how='left').fillna(0)
s_cct['exp_p'] = s_cct.s*s_cct.pps
s_cct['exp_c'] = s_cct.s*s_cct.cps
E = s_cct.groupby(['city','category_id'])[['exp_p','exp_c','s']].sum()

act_p = P.groupby(['city','category_id']).size().rename('act_p')
act_c = C.groupby(['city','category_id']).size().rename('act_c')
D = pd.concat([E, act_p, act_c], axis=1).fillna(0).reset_index()
D['p_purch'] = poisson.cdf(D.act_p, D.exp_p)
D['p_click'] = poisson.cdf(D.act_c, D.exp_c)
D = D.merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')

DD = D[D.exp_p>=5].sort_values('p_purch')
pd.set_option('display.width',220)
print(DD.head(30)[['city','category_name','s','act_c','exp_c','act_p','exp_p','p_click','p_purch']].to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.4g}"))
print("\nsorted p_purch (top 30):")
print(np.sort(DD.p_purch.values)[:30])
print("\nN tested:", len(DD))
D.to_pickle('/tmp/cond_search.pkl')
EOF
arguments.description: Test purchases conditioned on search activity per pair

# global purchases-per-search and clicks-per-search per (type, category)

p_tc = P.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('p')
c_tc = C.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('c')

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np

D = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/cond_search.pkl')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
pmap = dict(zip(cats.category_id, cats.parent_category_id))
pname = dict(zip(cats.category_id, cats.category_name))
D['parent'] = D.category_id.map(pmap).map(lambda p: pname.get(p,'root'))
D['ratio_c'] = D.act_c/D.exp_c.replace(0,np.nan)
D['ratio_p'] = D.act_p/D.exp_p.replace(0,np.nan)

pd.set_option('display.width',250)
for city in ['Madrid','Barcelona','Valencia','Sevilla','Zaragoza','Bilbao','Oviedo']:
    sub = D[(D.city==city)&(D.exp_c>=8)].sort_values('p_click')
    print(f"\n=== {city} (pairs with ratio_c<0.75 or p<0.01) ===")
    show = sub[(sub.ratio_c<0.75)|(sub.p_click<0.01)]
    print(show[['category_name','parent','s','act_c','exp_c','ratio_c','act_p','exp_p','ratio_p','p_click','p_purch']].to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.3g}"))
EOF

raw_arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np

D = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/cond_search.pkl')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
pmap = dict(zip(cats.category_id, cats.parent_category_id))
pname = dict(zip(cats.category_id, cats.category_name))
D['parent'] = D.category_id.map(pmap).map(lambda p: pname.get(p,'root'))
D['ratio_c'] = D.act_c/D.exp_c.replace(0,np.nan)
D['ratio_p'] = D.act_p/D.exp_p.replace(0,np.nan)

pd.set_option('display.width',250)
for city in ['Madrid','Barcelona','Valencia','Sevilla','Zaragoza','Bilbao','Oviedo']:
    sub = D[(D.city==city)&(D.exp_c>=8)].sort_values('p_click')
    print(f"\n=== {city} (pairs with ratio_c<0.75 or p<0.01) ===")
    show = sub[(sub.ratio_c<0.75)|(sub.p_click<0.01)]
    print(show[['category_name','parent','s','act_c','exp_c','ratio_c','act_p','exp_p','ratio_p','p_click','p_purch']].to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.3g}"))
EOF
raw_arguments.description: List suppressed categories per implicated city with parent groups

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np
from scipy.stats import poisson, combine_pvalues

D = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/cond_search.pkl')   # has act_c, exp_c (clicks|searches), act_p, exp_p (purch|searches)
A = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/agg.pkl')           # purchases conditioned on clicks: clicks, purch, expected
A = A.rename(columns={'expected':'exp_p_click','purch':'act_p2'})

M = D.merge(A[['city','category_id','exp_p_click','act_p2']], on=['city','category_id'], how='left').fillna(0)
M['p1'] = poisson.cdf(M.act_c, M.exp_c)          # click deficit given searches
M['p2'] = poisson.cdf(M.act_p2, M.exp_p_click)   # purchase deficit given clicks
M['p_fisher'] = [combine_pvalues([a,b])[1] for a,b in zip(M.p1, M.p2)]

MM = M[(M.exp_c>=8)].sort_values('p_fisher')
MM['rc'] = MM.act_c/MM.exp_c
pd.set_option('display.width',240)
print(MM.head(40)[['city','category_name','s','act_c','exp_c','rc','act_p','exp_p_click','p1','p2','p_fisher']].to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.3g}"))
print("\nlog10 p_fisher sorted (top 45):")
print(np.round(np.log10(np.sort(MM.p_fisher.values)[:45]),1))
print("\nN tested:", len(MM))
MM.to_pickle('/tmp/combined.pkl')
EOF
arguments.description: Fisher-combine click|search and purchase|click deficits

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np

M = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/combined.pkl')

def city_profile(cat_name):
    sub = M[M.category_name==cat_name].copy()
    sub['rc'] = sub.act_c/sub.exp_c
    sub['rp'] = (sub.act_p/sub.exp_p).round(2)
    sub = sub.sort_values('rc')
    return sub[['city','s','act_c','exp_c','rc','act_p','exp_p','p_fisher']]

for cat in ["Women's Clothing","Pets","Art","Furniture","Computers","Bags","Baby"]:
    sub = city_profile(cat)
    vals = sub.rc.round(2).tolist()
    cities = sub.city.tolist()
    print(f"\n{cat}: click|search ratio across cities (sorted)")
    print("  ", ", ".join(f"{c}:{v}" for c,v in zip(cities[:8], vals[:8])), " ... median:", round(np.median(vals),2))
    lo = sub[sub.rc<0.75]
    if len(lo): print(lo.to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.3g}"))
EOF
arguments.description: Compare per-city suppression profiles across categories

raw_arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np

M = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/combined.pkl')

def city_profile(cat_name):
    sub = M[M.category_name==cat_name].copy()
    sub['rc'] = sub.act_c/sub.exp_c
    sub['rp'] = (sub.act_p/sub.exp_p).round(2)
    sub = sub.sort_values('rc')
    return sub[['city','s','act_c','exp_c','rc','act_p','exp_p','p_fisher']]

for cat in ["Women's Clothing","Pets","Art","Furniture","Computers","Bags","Baby"]:
    sub = city_profile(cat)
    vals = sub.rc.round(2).tolist()
    cities = sub.city.tolist()
    print(f"\n{cat}: click|search ratio across cities (sorted)")
    print("  ", ", ".join(f"{c}:{v}" for c,v in zip(cities[:8], vals[:8])), " ... median:", round(np.median(vals),2))
    lo = sub[sub.rc<0.75]
    if len(lo): print(lo.to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.3g}"))
EOF
raw_arguments.description: Compare per-city suppression profiles across categories

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np
from scipy.stats import poisson

users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
fav = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/fav.pkl')
M = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/combined.pkl')

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)
Fv = fav[fav.city.isin(big)]

# favorites per search benchmark (type, category)
import glob
searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')
S = searches[searches.city.isin(big)]
s_tc = S.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('s')
f_tc = Fv.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('f')
G = pd.concat([s_tc,f_tc],axis=1).fillna(0)
G['fps'] = G.f/G.s

s_cct = S.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size().rename('s').reset_index()
s_cct = s_cct.merge(G[['fps']].reset_index(), on=['user_type','category_id'], how='left').fillna(0)
s_cct['exp_f'] = s_cct.s*s_cct.fps
Ef = s_cct.groupby(['city','category_id'])['exp_f'].sum()
act_f = Fv.groupby(['city','category_id']).size().rename('act_f')
FT = pd.concat([Ef, act_f], axis=1).fillna(0)
FT['p_fav'] = poisson.cdf(FT.act_f, FT.exp_f)

M2 = M.set_index(['city','category_id']).join(FT)
M2['rf'] = M2.act_f/M2.exp_f.replace(0,np.nan)

candidates = [
 ('Madrid','Pets'),('Madrid','Fitness'),('Madrid','Garden'),
 ('Barcelona','Art'),('Barcelona','Decor'),('Barcelona','Books'),
 ('Valencia','Furniture'),('Valencia','Tools'),('Valencia','Bicycles'),
 ('Sevilla','Computers'),('Sevilla','Consoles'),('Sevilla','Video Games'),
 ('Zaragoza','Musical Instruments'),('Zaragoza','Cameras'),('Zaragoza','Collectibles'),
 ('Bilbao','Bags'),("Bilbao","Women's Shoes"),('Bilbao','Formalwear'),
 ('Oviedo','Baby'),
 # borderline
 ('Malaga',"Women's Clothing"),('Valencia','Personal Care'),('Valencia','Art'),
 ('Zaragoza',"Children's Clothing"),('Zaragoza','Strollers'),
 ('Oviedo','Fishing'),('Oviedo','Navigation'),('Bilbao','Beauty'),('Bilbao','Photography'),('Bilbao','Lighting'),
 ('Las Palmas','Computers'),('Las Palmas','Kitchen'),('Santander','Kitchen'),
 ('Vigo','Video Games'),('Granada',"Women's Clothing"),('Cordoba','Hiking'),('Gijon','Movies'),('Burgos','Helmets'),
]
M3 = M2.reset_index().set_index(['city','category_name'])
print(f"{'city':12s} {'category':20s} {'rc':>5s} {'rf':>5s} {'rp':>5s} {'p_fav':>8s} {'p_fisher':>9s}")
for c,cat in candidates:
    r = M3.loc[(c,cat)]
    rp = r.act_p/r.exp_p if r.exp_p>0 else np.nan
    print(f"{c:12s} {cat:20s} {r.act_c/r.exp_c:5.2f} {r.rf:5.2f} {rp:5.2f} {r.p_fav:8.2g} {r.p_fisher:9.2g}")
EOF

raw_arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np
from scipy.stats import poisson

users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
fav = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/fav.pkl')
M = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/combined.pkl')

city_counts = users.city.value_counts()
big = set(city_counts[city_counts>=50].index)
Fv = fav[fav.city.isin(big)]

# favorites per search benchmark (type, category)
import glob
searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')
S = searches[searches.city.isin(big)]
s_tc = S.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('s')
f_tc = Fv.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size().rename('f')
G = pd.concat([s_tc,f_tc],axis=1).fillna(0)
G['fps'] = G.f/G.s

s_cct = S.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size().rename('s').reset_index()
s_cct = s_cct.merge(G[['fps']].reset_index(), on=['user_type','category_id'], how='left').fillna(0)
s_cct['exp_f'] = s_cct.s*s_cct.fps
Ef = s_cct.groupby(['city','category_id'])['exp_f'].sum()
act_f = Fv.groupby(['city','category_id']).size().rename('act_f')
FT = pd.concat([Ef, act_f], axis=1).fillna(0)
FT['p_fav'] = poisson.cdf(FT.act_f, FT.exp_f)

M2 = M.set_index(['city','category_id']).join(FT)
M2['rf'] = M2.act_f/M2.exp_f.replace(0,np.nan)

candidates = [
 ('Madrid','Pets'),('Madrid','Fitness'),('Madrid','Garden'),
 ('Barcelona','Art'),('Barcelona','Decor'),('Barcelona','Books'),
 ('Valencia','Furniture'),('Valencia','Tools'),('Valencia','Bicycles'),
 ('Sevilla','Computers'),('Sevilla','Consoles'),('Sevilla','Video Games'),
 ('Zaragoza','Musical Instruments'),('Zaragoza','Cameras'),('Zaragoza','Collectibles'),
 ('Bilbao','Bags'),("Bilbao","Women's Shoes"),('Bilbao','Formalwear'),
 ('Oviedo','Baby'),
 # borderline
 ('Malaga',"Women's Clothing"),('Valencia','Personal Care'),('Valencia','Art'),
 ('Zaragoza',"Children's Clothing"),('Zaragoza','Strollers'),
 ('Oviedo','Fishing'),('Oviedo','Navigation'),('Bilbao','Beauty'),('Bilbao','Photography'),('Bilbao','Lighting'),
 ('Las Palmas','Computers'),('Las Palmas','Kitchen'),('Santander','Kitchen'),
 ('Vigo','Video Games'),('Granada',"Women's Clothing"),('Cordoba','Hiking'),('Gijon','Movies'),('Burgos','Helmets'),
]
M3 = M2.reset_index().set_index(['city','category_name'])
print(f"{'city':12s} {'category':20s} {'rc':>5s} {'rf':>5s} {'rp':>5s} {'p_fav':>8s} {'p_fisher':>9s}")
for c,cat in candidates:
    r = M3.loc[(c,cat)]
    rp = r.act_p/r.exp_p if r.exp_p>0 else np.nan
    print(f"{c:12s} {cat:20s} {r.act_c/r.exp_c:5.2f} {r.rf:5.2f} {rp:5.2f} {r.p_fav:8.2g} {r.p_fisher:9.2g}")
EOF

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob
from scipy.stats import poisson, combine_pvalues

users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
fav = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/fav.pkl')
searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')

big = set(users.city.value_counts().pipe(lambda v: v[v>=50]).index)
S = searches[searches.city.isin(big)]; C = clicks[clicks.city.isin(big)]
P = purch[purch.city.isin(big)]; Fv = fav[fav.city.isin(big)]

def counts(df): return df.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size()
def gcounts(df): return df.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size()

s_g, c_g, p_g, f_g = gcounts(S), gcounts(C), gcounts(P), gcounts(Fv)
cps = (c_g/s_g).fillna(0)   # clicks per search
ppc = (p_g/c_g).fillna(0)   # purchases per click
fpc = (f_g/c_g).fillna(0)   # favorites per click

base = counts(S).rename('s').to_frame().join(counts(C).rename('c'), how='outer') \
    .join(counts(P).rename('p'), how='outer').join(counts(Fv).rename('f'), how='outer').fillna(0).reset_index()
base['exp_c'] = base.apply(lambda r: r.s*cps.get((r.user_type,r.category_id),0), axis=1)
base['exp_p'] = base.apply(lambda r: r.c*ppc.get((r.user_type,r.category_id),0), axis=1)
base['exp_f'] = base.apply(lambda r: r.c*fpc.get((r.user_type,r.category_id),0), axis=1)

agg = base.groupby(['city','category_id'])[['s','c','p','f','exp_c','exp_p','exp_f']].sum().reset_index()
agg['p_c'] = poisson.cdf(agg.c, agg.exp_c)
agg['p_p'] = poisson.cdf(agg.p, agg.exp_p)
agg['p_f'] = poisson.cdf(agg.f, agg.exp_f)
agg['score'] = [combine_pvalues([a,b,c])[1] for a,b,c in zip(agg.p_c, agg.p_p, agg.p_f)]
agg = agg.merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')

R = agg[agg.exp_c>=8].sort_values('score')
R['rc']=(R.c/R.exp_c).round(2); R['rf']=(R.f/R.exp_f.replace(0,np.nan)).round(2); R['rp']=(R.p/R.exp_p.replace(0,np.nan)).round(2)
pd.set_option('display.width',240)
print(R.head(32)[['city','category_name','s','c','exp_c','rc','f','rf','p','rp','p_c','p_f','p_p','score']].to_string(index=False, float_format=lambda x: f"{x:.3g}"))
print("\nlog10 score sorted (top 40):")
print(np.round(np.log10(np.sort(R.score.values)[:40]),1))
print("N:", len(R))
EOF
arguments.description: Final combined score over three independent funnel stages

python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob
from scipy.stats import poisson, combine_pvalues

# rebuild R quickly from previous cell logic (saved nothing) - redo
users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
fav = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/fav.pkl')
searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')

big = set(users.city.value_counts().pipe(lambda v: v[v>=50]).index)
S = searches[searches.city.isin(big)]; C = clicks[clicks.city.isin(big)]
P = purch[purch.city.isin(big)]; Fv = fav[fav.city.isin(big)]

def counts(df): return df.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size()
def gcounts(df): return df.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size()
s_g, c_g, p_g, f_g = gcounts(S), gcounts(C), gcounts(P), gcounts(Fv)
cps = (c_g/s_g).fillna(0); ppc = (p_g/c_g).fillna(0); fpc = (f_g/c_g).fillna(0)

base = counts(S).rename('s').to_frame().join(counts(C).rename('c'), how='outer') \
    .join(counts(P).rename('p'), how='outer').join(counts(Fv).rename('f'), how='outer').fillna(0).reset_index()
base['exp_c'] = [s*cps.get((t,k),0) for s,t,k in zip(base.s,base.user_type,base.category_id)]
base['exp_p'] = [c*ppc.get((t,k),0) for c,t,k in zip(base.c,base.user_type,base.category_id)]
base['exp_f'] = [c*fpc.get((t,k),0) for c,t,k in zip(base.c,base.user_type,base.category_id)]
agg = base.groupby(['city','category_id'])[['s','c','p','f','exp_c','exp_p','exp_f']].sum().reset_index()
agg['p_c'] = poisson.cdf(agg.c, agg.exp_c)
agg['p_p'] = poisson.cdf(agg.p, agg.exp_p)
agg['p_f'] = poisson.cdf(agg.f, agg.exp_f)
agg['score'] = [combine_pvalues([a,b,c])[1] for a,b,c in zip(agg.p_c, agg.p_p, agg.p_f)]
agg = agg.merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')
agg.to_pickle('/tmp/final_scores.pkl')

R = agg[agg.exp_c>=8]
print("Per-city best 3 scores (sorted by city size):")
order = users.city.value_counts()
for city in [c for c in order.index if c in big]:
    sub = R[R.city==city].nsmallest(4,'score')
    items = ", ".join(f"{r.category_name}={r.score:.1.0e}" if False else f"{r.category_name}={r.score:.0e}" for _,r in sub.iterrows())
    print(f"{city:14s} ({order[city]:4d} users): {items}")
EOF

raw_arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob
from scipy.stats import poisson, combine_pvalues

# rebuild R quickly from previous cell logic (saved nothing) - redo
users = pd.read_csv('user_001.csv')
cats = pd.read_csv('category_001.csv')
purch = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/purch.pkl')
clicks = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/clicks.pkl')
fav = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/fav.pkl')
searches = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('search_event_*.csv'))])
searches = searches.merge(users[['user_id','user_type','city']], on='user_id')

big = set(users.city.value_counts().pipe(lambda v: v[v>=50]).index)
S = searches[searches.city.isin(big)]; C = clicks[clicks.city.isin(big)]
P = purch[purch.city.isin(big)]; Fv = fav[fav.city.isin(big)]

def counts(df): return df.groupby(['city','category_id','user_type']).size()
def gcounts(df): return df.groupby(['user_type','category_id']).size()
s_g, c_g, p_g, f_g = gcounts(S), gcounts(C), gcounts(P), gcounts(Fv)
cps = (c_g/s_g).fillna(0); ppc = (p_g/c_g).fillna(0); fpc = (f_g/c_g).fillna(0)

base = counts(S).rename('s').to_frame().join(counts(C).rename('c'), how='outer') \
    .join(counts(P).rename('p'), how='outer').join(counts(Fv).rename('f'), how='outer').fillna(0).reset_index()
base['exp_c'] = [s*cps.get((t,k),0) for s,t,k in zip(base.s,base.user_type,base.category_id)]
base['exp_p'] = [c*ppc.get((t,k),0) for c,t,k in zip(base.c,base.user_type,base.category_id)]
base['exp_f'] = [c*fpc.get((t,k),0) for c,t,k in zip(base.c,base.user_type,base.category_id)]
agg = base.groupby(['city','category_id'])[['s','c','p','f','exp_c','exp_p','exp_f']].sum().reset_index()
agg['p_c'] = poisson.cdf(agg.c, agg.exp_c)
agg['p_p'] = poisson.cdf(agg.p, agg.exp_p)
agg['p_f'] = poisson.cdf(agg.f, agg.exp_f)
agg['score'] = [combine_pvalues([a,b,c])[1] for a,b,c in zip(agg.p_c, agg.p_p, agg.p_f)]
agg = agg.merge(cats[['category_id','category_name']], on='category_id')
agg.to_pickle('/tmp/final_scores.pkl')

R = agg[agg.exp_c>=8]
print("Per-city best 3 scores (sorted by city size):")
order = users.city.value_counts()
for city in [c for c in order.index if c in big]:
    sub = R[R.city==city].nsmallest(4,'score')
    items = ", ".join(f"{r.category_name}={r.score:.1.0e}" if False else f"{r.category_name}={r.score:.0e}" for _,r in sub.iterrows())
    print(f"{city:14s} ({order[city]:4d} users): {items}")
EOF
raw_arguments.description: Per-city best scores to calibrate borderline pairs

steps.message:
Done — the answer is written to `/app/answer.json`:

```json
{"Barcelona": ["Art", "Books", "Decor"], "Bilbao": ["Bags", "Formalwear", "Women's Shoes"], "Madrid": ["Fitness", "Garden", "Pets"], "Malaga": ["Women's Clothing"], "Oviedo": ["Baby"], "Sevilla": ["Computers", "Consoles", "Video Games"], "Valencia": ["Bicycles", "Furniture", "Tools"], "Zaragoza": ["Cameras", "Collectibles", "Musical Instruments"]}
```

**How I got there.** The data is a full marketplace funnel: searches → impressions → clicks → favorites → purchases, joined to users (city, user_type) and listings (category). Key methodological choices:

- **Non-negligible cities:** 30 cities have ~100–1,500 users; the other 50 "cities" have exactly 1 user each and were excluded.
- **User types matter:** business users are predominantly sellers (5% click→purchase conversion vs ~25–27% for regular/verified), so all benchmarks were computed per user type and per category, then aggregated to each city using its own type mix — otherwise type composition would confound city comparisons.
- **The right signal is conversion of activity, not raw volume.** Many city/category pairs have low purchases simply because local search interest is low (ordinary taste variation — campaigns can't claim "activity strongly suggests" potential there). The anomalous pairs are the opposite profile: search activity at or above the expected level, but everything downstream — clicks, favorites, purchases — suppressed to roughly 30–60% of what the same activity yields in other cities. (The 2-month auto-expiry also means supply isn't the bottleneck: ~63% of listings expire unsold, so the deficit is demand-side conversion.)
- **Detection:** for each (city, category) I computed Poisson lower-tail p-values for three near-independent funnel stages — clicks given searches, favorites given clicks, purchases given clicks — benchmarked on all other cities (per user type), and Fisher-combined them.

The separation is unambiguous: 20 pairs score between 1e-24 and 1.3e-3, while the best score among all ~1,700 remaining pairs in the other 22 cities is ~9e-3. The structure itself is telling — six big cities have exactly three affected categories each (e.g., all three are Electronics in Sevilla, all three Fashion in Bilbao), plus two single-category outliers: Oviedo–Baby (clicks at 7% of expected) and Malaga–Women's Clothing (clicks 70%, but favorites 52% and purchases 35% of expected). These are exactly the city/category pairs where users demonstrably look for items but transact far less than identical activity produces elsewhere — the right targets for notifications, better recommendations, or free-delivery offers.
